builtins.name: log
builtins.summary: Log a message and store the expanded message as the last execution result.

notes: Expansions resolve through normal runtime path lookup.

builtins.name: respond
builtins.summary: Return an HTTP-style response from a step sequence.

builtins.name: parse-json
builtins.summary: Parse JSON from a source variable, defaulting to body.

builtins.name: validate
builtins.summary: Validate data against conditions or schema-driven expectations.

builtins.name: csv.read
builtins.summary: Read CSV data from disk into runtime context.

builtins.name: csv.write
builtins.summary: Write CSV data from runtime context to disk.

builtins.name: csv.append
builtins.summary: Append a record to a CSV file.

builtins.name: json.read
builtins.summary: Read JSON data from disk into runtime context.

builtins.name: delete
builtins.summary: Remove a variable or nested path from runtime context.

builtins.name: is-set
builtins.summary: Check if a variable or path exists in context, assigns boolean result.

builtins.name: datasource
builtins.summary: Access configured datasource behavior.

builtins.name: load-rune
builtins.summary: Load another Rune document or directory of Rune files, resolving top-level imports before parsing.

builtins.name: memory.set
builtins.summary: Persist a value in shared memory.

builtins.name: memory.get
builtins.summary: Retrieve a value from shared memory.

builtins.name: memory.clear
builtins.summary: Clear memory entries.

builtins.name: memory.del
builtins.summary: Delete a memory entry.

builtins.name: append
builtins.summary: Append data to a collection-like value.

builtins.name: ws.id
builtins.summary: Access the current websocket connection id.

builtins.name: ws.send
builtins.summary: Send a websocket message to a target connection.

notes: The `ws_id` argument may be either a literal connection id or a context variable that resolves to the current target connection id.
If the `message` argument resolves to a structured value in context, it is serialized to JSON before sending.

builtins.name: ws.broadcast
builtins.summary: Broadcast a websocket message.

notes: Slash-prefixed websocket paths such as /ws are treated as normal command arguments.
